semantics.rule: A stage adds memory capabilities only. It never authorizes goal management, planning, tool execution, external effects, or task orchestration.

known_operation.rule: Resolve the operation to its declared memory capability and compare its minimum stage with the accepted deployment stage.

unknown_operation.rule: An unknown, malformed, unclassified, or non-memory operation is denied before processing or side effects.

configuration.rule: Configuration, policy, approval, request content, consumer correlation, prompt, or model output cannot change the accepted stage or enable a denied capability.

constraints: Catalog objects carry their own identifier and required parent lineage, never descendants; Brain ancestry below Tenant is resolved transitively.
Tenant does not carry area_id.
Operational memory always carries tenant_id and area_id.
Sentinel, nullable required, implicit, and payload-derived scope are denied.
Only the Memory Transition Gate writes protected memory state.
Every record has authenticated immutable scope, governance metadata, and provenance.
Raw working context never crosses an Area boundary.
Candidates are operational work items, not active memories.
Candidates are excluded from normal retrieval until controlled Stage 3 promotion.
Persistence alone never activates a candidate.
Stage 1 is dry-run only and cannot activate a successor or change an active pointer.
Stage 2 may analyze durable memory but cannot promote or activate.
Stage 3 owns controlled consolidation, independent validation, promotion, quarantine, and rollback.
Stage 4 cross-Area output is minimized candidate-only under a separate dual-scope transfer contract.
Every run requires an inactive Area, exclusive valid lease, immutable current snapshot, provenance, policy, and audit.

scope: retention metadata and legal hold foundation
constraints: Immutable means unchanged until policy-compliant deletion or anonymization.
Stage 1 establishes the deletion foundation; dependency-aware deletion and anonymization execute only from Stage 2.
Secrets and credentials are excluded.
Retrieval returns provenance and freshness.
Stage 2 does not authorize candidate promotion or procedural learning.
Unknown-freshness content is not represented as current fact.
Sensitive or risky candidates preserve producer-promoter separation.
Promotion creates a new version and preserves source provenance.
Procedural memory is information for consumers and cannot execute a procedure.
Distributed operation preserves single protected-transition ownership, audit atomicity, and fail-closed recovery.
No raw memory, working context, authority, or implicit access crosses an Area boundary.
Any cross-Area handover requires a separately accepted explicit contract and dual-scope authorization; Stage 4 alone does not create that authorization.

absolute_prohibitions: Neural Brain does not own goals, plans, tools, actions, external effects, or task-success decisions in any stage.
Raw memory and working context do not cross an Area boundary in any stage.
A later stage cannot override default deny, scope isolation, governance, the Memory Transition Gate, audit atomicity, privacy, retention, or deletion.
A candidate is not active memory merely because it was persisted.
A Dreaming worker or model cannot write protected memory, activate a candidate, or change an active-version pointer directly.
Dreaming does not run in an active Area and never mixes raw memory across Areas.
No configuration can enable a later-stage capability early.
